_comment: Copy-paste MCP configurations for connecting to the MCP Tools for Elementor server. Pick the one that matches your client and environment.

claude_desktop_local_wpcli._description: Claude Desktop — local WordPress via WP-CLI (fastest, recommended for local dev)

claude_desktop_remote_npx._description: Claude Desktop — remote WordPress via npx runner (RECOMMENDED for remote/shared hosting; nothing to install or keep in sync)
claude_desktop_remote_npx._note: The proxy runs as a LOCAL subprocess on the machine with Claude Desktop. npx fetches it on demand, so there is no file path to maintain. Requires Node.js 18+ locally.

env.WP_APP_PASSWORD: xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx

claude_desktop_remote_proxy_local_copy._description: Claude Desktop — remote WordPress via a LOCAL copy of the proxy file
claude_desktop_remote_proxy_local_copy._note: Claude Desktop launches the proxy locally, so mcp-proxy.mjs must exist on THIS machine, not on the server. Extract bin/mcp-proxy.mjs from the plugin ZIP, save it locally, and point 'args' at that local path. Re-extract after plugin updates to get proxy fixes.

env.WP_APP_PASSWORD: xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx

claude_code_remote_npx._description: Claude Code — remote WordPress via npx runner with protocol version override
claude_code_remote_npx._note: The npx package runs locally; no file path to maintain. Requires Node.js 18+.

env.WP_APP_PASSWORD: xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx

claude_code_mcp_json._description: Claude Code — place as .mcp.json in project root

claude_ai_custom_connector._description: Claude.ai (web) — custom connector using URL-based authentication
claude_ai_custom_connector._note: Claude.ai's custom connector UI only accepts a server URL, with no field for an Authorization header, and the connection runs from Anthropic's cloud so there's no local client to add one either. Enable 'URL-based authentication' on the Connection tab first (off by default), then use this URL as the connector's server URL.

vscode_mcp_direct_http._description: VS Code MCP extension — direct HTTP connection (add to VS Code settings)

mcp_inspector_wpcli._description: MCP Inspector — test and debug tools via WP-CLI

mcp_inspector_proxy._description: MCP Inspector — test and debug tools via HTTP proxy
mcp_inspector_proxy._command: npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector -e WP_URL=https://your-site.com -e WP_USERNAME=admin -e WP_APP_PASSWORD=xxxx node bin/mcp-proxy.mjs
